data.title: Kestra Medical Technologies, Ltd. GAAP EPS of -$0.64 misses by $0.05, revenue of $22.56M beats by $1.6M
data.description:
• Kestra Medical Technologies, Ltd. press release (KMTS): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.64 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $22.56M (+53.4% Y/Y) beats by $1.6M.
  • Kestra is increasing its FY26 revenue guidance to $91 million, which would represent growth of 52% compared to FY25. This compares to prior FY26 revenue guidance of $88 million and initial FY26 guidance of $85 million.

data.title: Quanex Building Products Non-GAAP EPS of $0.83 beats by $0.31, revenue of $489.8M beats by $19.06M
data.description:
• Quanex Building Products press release (NX): FQ4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.83 beats by $0.31.
  • Revenue of $489.8M (-0.5% Y/Y) beats by $19.06M.
  • Shares +11.34%.
  • $75 Million of Debt Repaid in Fiscal 2025Cash Provided by Operating Activities Increased ~86% Year-Over-YearHealthy Balance Sheet and Strong LiquidityIntegration of Transformative Acquisition Substantially Complete and Thesis Remains Intact

  • Outlook        

    Mr. Wilson stated, “Our long-term view continues to be favorable as the underlying fundamentals for the residential housing market remain positive. We enter fiscal 2026 with a cautious outlook due to the ongoing macroeconomic challenges, but we are optimistic that demand for our products will improve as consumer confidence is restored over time. Our current view is that fiscal 2026 could be flat compared to fiscal 2025 from a revenue and EBITDA perspective, with puts and takes, but the first half of 2026 may be more challenged than the first half of 2025, which would imply a somewhat improved second half year-over-year.   Having said that, and consistent with the last few years, based on current macro indicators, recent conversations with our customers, limited transparency, and varying opinions on the macroeconomic outlook for 2026, we are again taking a measured approach to guidance. We believe it would be premature to give official guidance at this time and intend to re-visit guidance for 2026 when we report earnings for the first quarter. As macroeconomic uncertainty subsides and consumer confidence improves, we believe our team is well positioned to capitalize on pent-up demand. In the meantime, we will stay focused on the things that we can control, with an emphasis on generating cash to continue paying down debt and opportunistically repurchasing our stock.”

data.title: S&P and Dow end at record levels, Oracle pulls Nasdaq lower
data.description:
Wall Street traded watched both the blue-chip Dow and the benchmark S&P 500 notch record closes while the tech-oriented Nasdaq Composite dragged lower after Oracle (ORCL) posted mixed results, pressuring the AI trade.

The Dow (DJI) was the best performer as it closed higher by 1.3% at 48,704.01. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finished up by 0.2% at 6,900.99 and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) handed back 0.2% and ended at 23,593.86.

Across sectors, eight of the 11 S&P groups closed in positive territory and were led by Materials. At the other end of the spectrum, Communication Services was been the weakest performer.

Investors also continued to react to Wednesday’s Fed decision to cut its policy rate by 25 basis points to 3.50%-3.75%, which is now its lowest level in three years. 

"The rotation continues. After yesterday’s press conference, the market has realized the Fed is willing to ‘run it hot.’ This includes higher inflation risks and the high probability of much higher nominal growth. When adding the increasing pressure on AI-related CapEx spending, the market seems primed for a longer-term rotation into value stocks like cyclical value and higher-yielding dividend stocks, which I expect to give the equal-weight S&P 500 an edge over the market-weighted version," Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen  stated.

“The Federal Reserve sent a clear message that the Fed is in the midst of an accommodative phase, largely driven by the recent weakness in the labor market. While 2026 may not bring as many rate cuts as 2025, it's clear that the Fed's prior hiking cycle that disrupted markets and the economy post-Covid is firmly in the rearview mirror, and that's bullish for stocks,” Chris Kampitsis, managing partner of Barnum Financial Group said. 

Furthermore, Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid stated: “Bond yields have also moved lower, which partly reflects the Fed and the wider risk-off tone this morning.” Reid noted Japan’s robust bond auction and Australia’s unexpected employment contraction as contributing factors. 

Yields moved lower as the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.53% and the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 1 basis point to 4.15%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Oracle (ORCL) dropped 10.9% while shares of Mosaic (MOS) pushed up by 6.1%.

data.title: Top homebuilder stocks as Powell says no tools to address a 'structural housing shortage'
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Thursday expressed some concerns about the U.S. housing market and said that the central bank doesn't "really have the tools to address" a housing shortage. 

Powell's remarks came at a press conference on Wednesday after the Fed cut its key policy rate by 25 basis points for a third straight meeting. 

"The housing market faces some really significant challenges. And I don't know that, you know, a 25-basis point decline in the federal funds rate is going to make much of a difference for people," the Fed chair said. 

"Housing supply is low. Many people have very, very low-rate mortgages from the pandemic period and they kept refinancing and caught the really low. So it's made it expensive for them to move. And, you know, we're a ways away from that changing," Powell added. 

Home affordability remains an issue in the U.S. The median sales price of houses sold in Q2 2025 was $410,800, just 7.7% lower than the record $442,600 in Q4 2022. Mortgage rates recently have remained well below their YTD averages, according to Freddie Mac (FMCC). 

"We haven't built enough housing in the country for a long time. And so a lot of estimates suggest that we just need more housing of different kinds. So housing is going to be a -- you know, a problem," Fed's Powell said.

"We can raise and lower interest rates, but we don't really have the tools to address...a structural housing shortage," he added.

In light of the central bank chief's comments, Seeking Alpha compiled a list of top homebuilder stocks. This list includes companies specializing in residential construction, from single-family and multi-family to custom home construction, serving diverse housing markets. 

Cavco Industries (CVCO), Green Brick Partners (GRBK), and Champion Homes (SKY) top the list with Quant Ratings above 4.0, making them the only “Buy” rated homebuilders according to the Seeking Alpha system. Installed Building Products (IBP) and Century Communities (CCS) round out the top five.

Notable large-cap builders like D.R. Horton (DHI), Lennar (LEN), and PulteGroup (PHM) all received “Hold” ratings, while Dream Finders Homes (DFH) and LGI Homes (LGIH) sit at the bottom with “Strong Sell” ratings.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system grades stocks based on their performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, profitability, and momentum metrics. Each stock is rated on a scale of 1 to 5, with any rating above 3.5 considered a bullish rating. A score of 2.5 or below represents a bearish profile for the stock.

Here is the complete list of homebuilder stocks:

  1. Cavco Industries (CVCO), Quant Rating: 4.39
  2. Green Brick Partners (GRBK), Quant Rating: 4.09
  3. Champion Homes (SKY), Quant Rating: 4.02
  4. Installed Building Products (IBP), Quant Rating: 3.29
  5. Century Communities (CCS), Quant Rating: 3.22
  6. Tri Pointe Homes (TPH), Quant Rating: 3.03
  7. PulteGroup (PHM), Quant Rating: 2.94
  8. Toll Brothers (TOL), Quant Rating: 2.93
  9. NVR (NVR), Quant Rating: 2.90
  10. TopBuild (BLD), Quant Rating: 2.76
  11. KB Home (KBH), Quant Rating: 2.74
  12. D.R. Horton (DHI), Quant Rating: 2.74
  13. M/I Homes (MHO), Quant Rating: 2.73
  14. Taylor Morrison Home (TMHC), Quant Rating: 2.72
  15. Lennar (LEN), Quant Rating: 2.69
  16. Beazer Homes (BZH), Quant Rating: 2.67
  17. Hovnanian Enterprises (HOV), Quant Rating: 2.61
  18. Meritage Homes (MTH), Quant Rating: 2.10
  19. Smith Douglas Homes (SDHC), Quant Rating: 1.84
  20. Legacy Housing (LEGH), Quant Rating: 1.57
  21. LGI Homes (LGIH), Quant Rating: 1.29
  22. Dream Finders Homes (DFH), Quant Rating: 1.27

data.title: BlackRock said to sell 7% stake in Naturgy for $2B
data.description:
Naturgy Energy Group (GASNY) (GASNF) American depository receipts slid 1.9% in Thursday afternoon trading following a report that BlackRock (BLK) had shed its 7.1% stake in the Spanish gas utility for ~€1.7B ($1.99B).

The sale was executed through an accelerated bookbuild placement of 68.8M shares at €24.75 per share managed by JPMorgan (JPM), Reuters reported, citing a statement from one of the companies. That represents a discount of ~5.4% to Wednesday's closing price of €26.16.

BlackRock still owns an ~11.4% stake in Naturgy, the report said. The asset firm had acquired the Naturgy shares through its acquisition of Global Infrastructure Partners in 2024, which had previously invested in the company, the article said.

BlackRock (BLK) declined to comment about the transaction to Seeking Alpha. Naturgy (GASNY) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

With the sale, BlackRock is Naturgy's fourth-largest shareholder, after Spanish holding company Criteria, with an almost 24% stake, private equity firm CVC with an 18.6% stake, and Australian fund IFM with 15.2% ownership.
